type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Instead of considering all the generators on campus as one large 1.7 MW generator, I split them down into two categories (> 450 kW and < = 450 kW) as instructed in the NPRI calculation spreadsheet (which I should have done the previous year, too). The SO2 emission factor for large generators is 2.50E-02 lb/MMBTU and the one for smaller generators is 2.90E-01 lb/MMBTU.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Using the JTU NG Emissions Calculator, I entered the consumption data for the powerhouse boilers under "NSPS Large Wall-Fired Boilers (>100 MMBtu/h Heat Input)" in 2012 and corrected it to "Small Wall-Fired Boilers (<100 MMBtu/h Heat Input)" in 2013 because the total heat input capacity of the boilers at the powerhouse is 60 MMBTU/h.  Please advise whether we should revise the 2012 report.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The availability of diesel and fuel oil invoice records was limited due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Changes in methods of data retrieval and estimation were required for this submission year. Consumption data for previous submission years were pulled directly from invoices. For 2019 consumption data was pulled directly from invoices when possible, for missing invoices estimates were made from available financial data, known utility rates, and historical consumption.

There was a data entry error in the 2018 reporting year that amounted to 132,414 cubic meters of natural gas consumption to not be included within the 2018 greenhouse gas and pollutant calculations. With this error avoided in 2019, it consequently appears as a dramatic increase when compared to what was reported for 2018. In addition to this error, colder weather in 2019 contributed to increases in pollutant emissions as heating equipment consumed more fuel.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Change in emission factor for substance #7446-09-5 in the 2019 calculation spreadsheet for diesel-fired generators over 600 HP. The university purchases ultra low sulphur diesel for stationary generators which has an average sulphur content of 0.00033% in Québec (10 year average, 2003-2012).

The availability of diesel and fuel oil invoice records was limited due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Changes in methods of data retrieval and estimation were required for this submission year. Consumption data for previous submission years were pulled directly from invoices. For 2019 consumption data was pulled directly from invoices when possible, for missing invoices estimates were made from available financial data, known utility rates, and historical consumption.

comments.comment:
The availability of diesel and fuel oil invoice records was limited due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Changes in methods of data retrieval and estimation were required for this submission year. Consumption data for previous submission years were pulled directly from invoices. For 2019 consumption data was pulled directly from invoices when possible, for missing invoices estimates were made from available financial data, known utility rates, and historical consumption.

There was a data entry error in the 2018 reporting year that amounted to 132,414 cubic meters of natural gas consumption to not be included within the 2018 greenhouse gas and pollutant calculations. With this error avoided in 2019, it consequently appears as a dramatic increase when compared to what was reported for 2018. type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The 2019 methodologies within the calculation tools for liquid fuels provided by ECCC (Diesel fuel Generator - Fuel usage, Large stationary diesel engine (>600HP), and Fuel Oil Combustion - Boilers and Heaters) have changed. Although emissions factors remained the same, there are now more substances included/calculated as PAHs, therefore appearing as a significant increase in 2019. Isolating these new substances out, the actual change would not have been significant (less than 10% increase) in comparison to the previous year.  

The availability of diesel and fuel oil invoice records was limited due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Changes in methods of data retrieval and estimation were required for this submission year. Consumption data for previous submission years were pulled directly from invoices. For 2019 consumption data was pulled directly from invoices when possible, for missing invoices estimates were made from available financial data, known utility rates, and historical consumption.

There was a data entry error in the 2018 reporting year that amounted to 132,414 cubic meters of natural gas consumption to not be included within the 2018 greenhouse gas and pollutant calculations. With this error avoided in 2019, it consequently appears as a dramatic increase when compared to what was reported for 2018. In addition to this error, colder weather in 2019 contributed to increases in pollutant emissions as heating equipment consumed more fuel.

reasonsForNoActivities.fr: Autres (précisez dans le champ des commentaires)

p2Plan.noActivitiesComment: The University doesn't have a pollution prevention plan. However, the University has committed to reaching carbon neutrality in all its activities by 2040. The following documents include detailed actions to reduce energy consumption and airborne contaminants and reach zero carbon emissions by 2040 : the Roadmap for the Energy Transition, the Five-Year Energy Management Plan, and the Sustainability and Climate Action Plan. None of these documents per se are pollution prevention plans, but they go beyond pollution prevention and mitigation.
